that aimed at supporting students to be both college and career ready. We appreciate that the
district is on track for its CCMR goal and progress measures. These data are lagged and do not
reflect the recent data back changes the district has.

[25:30–26:00]
been implementing. In particular, I want to highlight the district's opt-out scheduling process,
which should allow more students to take advanced courses. Research shows that access to these
courses increases college completion, and maintaining an opt-out policy can be a critical tool for
ensuring equitable opportunities for all students. We also know that there is still work to be done
to eliminate achievement gaps that students face, and look forward to more information about how the
district is supporting specific populations of students, especially emergent bilingual and students
with disabilities who face the deepest gaps.
